behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real `git` rename signal plus CLI categorization. MOCK-MISS: this is not only local parsing logic; misconfigured `git diff`/`git log` flags can suppress rename detection and produce add+delete. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: requires real subprocess interaction with `git` rename semantics, which in-process mocks can hide. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text is about repository-derived rename classification, not just module handoff with fixed fixture input.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit a file, rename it (`git mv`), modify if needed, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows 1 rename and 0 corresponding add/delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real `git` diff output for binary changes and CLI subprocess decoding/parsing path. REAL-INFRA: behavior depends on real subprocess output shape for binary diffs. MOCK-HIDE: mocked text diff can miss actual binary diff markers/encoding edge cases. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires handling real binary-file diffs from the repository context.
behaviors.test_description: Create repo with a binary file (e.g., random bytes), commit, change binary content, run CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success and binary change is counted in report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real `git` behavior in an unborn-HEAD repo and CLI error handling of subprocess exit/status/output. REAL-INFRA: requires real repository state and subprocess return codes. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing git responses can mask actual no-commit edge behavior. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because the behavior text is specifically about a real repo state with no commits, which is OS/subprocess-facing.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new repo with no commits, run CLI subprocess in that directory, assert successful execution and an empty structured report (not an exception/error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real git history traversal, status extraction, and aggregation. MOCK-MISS: bugs can occur at subprocess/history query boundary, not just local counting logic. REAL-INFRA: requires real commit graph and git command behavior. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text requires matching actual repository history, not only module wiring with synthetic fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a controlled sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies, run CLI subprocess, and assert category counts equal the known ground truth from that commit sequence.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git index vs working-tree state handling and CLI query mode. REAL-INFRA: staged/unstaged semantics are git/OS stateful behavior. MOCK-HIDE: fakes can miss index/worktree differences that drive divergent outputs. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly compares real staged versus unstaged repository states.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create identical content changes once as unstaged and once staged (resetting between runs), execute CLI subprocess for each case, and assert identical structured output.
